probability.last_reasoning: Upgrading bear probability from 10% to 12% due to: compound stress approaching ELEVATED, extreme fear at 14, persistent data gaps preventing kill switch validation, and permissioned DEX silence post-launch. Slight shift from base to mid reflects RLUSD recovery ($1.584B) and continued institutional infrastructure buildout (Ripple Prime, Metallicus FedNow). Bull case trimmed marginally given macro headwinds. Net effect: modestly more uncertain distribution reflecting the tension between deteriorating macro and recovering fundamentals.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Elevated from baseline due to: persistent ODL data opacity (Ripple's choice to stop publishing is itself a signal), permissioned DEX showing zero adoption 11 days post-launch, extreme fear environment pressuring ETF flows, and compound macro stress approaching ELEVATED. The thesis infrastructure narrative remains intact but on-chain utility evidence gap is widening.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT continues upgrading GPI with pre-validation and tracking across 11,500 banks — network effect moat remains formidable. No XRPL integration signals.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Operating in parallel corridors but not directly competing for the same institutional settlement infrastructure XRP targets. Low immediate threat.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Kinexys (fka Onyx) processes significant daily volume on proprietary blockchain. JPMorgan's institutional relationships give it natural advantage in corridors where XRP seeks entry.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: BIS Nexus represents sovereign-backed alternative to private bridge currencies. If Nexus achieves multi-country fast payment linkage without bridge assets, it directly undermines XRP's core value proposition. Kill switch #9 active.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ethereum L2s and institutional DeFi growing but targeting different use cases (tokenization, DeFi) rather than cross-border settlement. Overlap exists in RWA tokenization where XRPL also competes.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data has been unavailable for an extended period — Ripple's deliberate opacity on its flagship utility metric is the single most concerning bear signal. If volume were growing impressively, there would be incentive to publish.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: Token velocity data remains unknown (kill switch #8). If XRP is used purely as a bridge currency with immediate conversion, utility usage may not translate to sustained price appreciation regardless of volume growth.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Extreme fear (14), deteriorating macro indicators, and compound stress approaching ELEVATED suggest rising recession probability that would suppress institutional crypto adoption timelines.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: F&G at 14 with XRP at $1.39 (well below ATH) shows market stress, but institutional infrastructure buildout continues regardless of sentiment — not yet a crypto winter pattern.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: 7 spot ETFs approved and live; Clarity Act pending. No signals of regulatory reversal. Current administration remains crypto-favorable.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 3.97% suggests rates stabilizing near 4%. BOJ rate action is the bigger risk — any hike could cascade through carry trade unwind, hitting all risk assets including XRP.

bear_case.bear_narrative: INCREASE MONITORING. Three kill switches at WARNING — ODL volume unmeasurable with suspicious absence, RLUSD at 32% of target with no acceleration visible, and ODL transparency unlikely to resolve given Ripple's behavioral pattern. The thesis's central paradox is sharpening: institutional capital is flowing in (7 ETFs, SBI deploying capital, Franklin Templeton signaling) while institutional infrastructure choices are flowing away (SWIFT→Chainlink, DTCC→CCIP, JPMorgan→Partior). Compound stress is pre-loaded with the BOJ March meeting as the next binary catalyst. The most honest assessment: we are monitoring a thesis we cannot currently falsify because the entity that controls the core data has chosen not to publish it — and that choice is itself the most important signal in the system.
